specific_mechanism: EikonalRoute: build a token×layer grid; learned per-token-per-layer speed v(t,l); solve discretized eikonal ‖∇u‖=1/v via fast marching to obtain arrival time u(t,L); route token t to early-exit layer ⌈u(t,L)⌉.
llm_application: EikonalRoute: per-token speed v(t,l) is computed from a learned MLP on hidden state h_l(t). Discretize a 2D token×layer grid (N_tokens × L). Run fast-marching (min-heap upwind FD) to obtain u(t,L) for all tokens. Tokens for which u(t,L) ≤ l exit at layer l (early-exit). Auxiliary loss matches u to actual exit decision.
novelty_claim: EikoNet (Smith 2020) solves eikonal with NN; Deep Eikonal Solvers extends. EikonalRoute as token-level routing PDE is more specific.
motivation_justification: Eikonal is a first-order PDE for shortest paths; token-layer grid is a 2D analog. Structural mapping is shared math, not exact mechanism transfer.
content_words: eikonal equation fast marching shortest path
fast marching min-heap upwind finite difference

source_side: eikonal equation fast marching shortest path
fast marching min-heap upwind finite difference
